prompt_md: You are the project manager for a proposed **AI ticket-triage** feature. The business wants it on the roadmap. You want to manage the inherent uncertainty of an AI feature instead of treating it like a normal fixed-scope build. **Select every option that is a sound way to run this as an AI project.**

choices: Agree a success metric and threshold up front (e.g. ≥85% correctly routed on a labelled sample) before any build
Run a short feasibility spike against real, messy data to measure quality before committing a launch date
Keep an AI risk register covering data, model-quality, cost-at-scale, and compliance risks, each with an owner
Define acceptance as eval scores over the threshold plus guardrails passing and cost within budget
Commit a fixed delivery date and scope before the spike, because the stakeholder needs certainty
Remove the kill option once the pilot is funded, since stopping would waste the money already spent
